required_skills: Flight test planning and test card development for aircraft or spacecraft systems
Flight test data acquisition system (DAS) design and instrumentation engineering
Aircraft performance and flying qualities assessment per MIL-F-8785C or MIL-STD-1797
Airworthiness and certification compliance testing per FAR Part 23/25 or DEF STAN 00-970
Risk assessment and hazard analysis for flight test operations
Telemetry system design and real-time flight data monitoring and analysis
Flight envelope expansion and clearance methodology
Structural loads measurement and strain gauge instrumentation for flight loads surveys
Handling qualities evaluation and Cooper-Harper rating scale application
Post-flight data reduction, analysis, and engineering report writing
preferred_skills: Experience with rotary-wing or tiltrotor aircraft flight test programs
Knowledge of IRIG-106 telemetry standards and PCM/IRIG data encoding
Familiarity with flight test safety management systems (SMS) and flight test risk matrices
Experience with store compatibility and weapons integration flight testing
Knowledge of flutter and aeroelastic clearance flight test methods
Familiarity with UAV and unmanned systems flight test regulatory frameworks (FAA Part 107)
Experience with engine performance and propulsion system airborne testing
responsibilities: Develop comprehensive flight test plans, test cards, and safety risk assessments for aircraft performance, handling qualities, and systems integration test programs
Design and integrate flight test instrumentation packages including pressure transducers, accelerometers, strain gauges, and data acquisition systems on test articles
Monitor real-time telemetry data streams during flight operations to assess test point compliance, vehicle health, and safety of flight parameters
Conduct quantitative and qualitative aircraft performance analyses including climb performance, range, ceiling, and specific excess power (Ps) across the flight envelope
Evaluate aircraft handling qualities and flying qualities against MIL-STD-1797 criteria and document Cooper-Harper pilot ratings from structured evaluation sorties
Plan and execute flight envelope expansion programs, progressively clearing the aircraft to structural and aerodynamic limits using incremental test point methodology
Perform post-flight data reduction and statistical analysis to validate system performance against specifications, generating detailed engineering flight test reports
Coordinate with airworthiness authorities (FAA, EASA, or military equivalents) to prepare and present compliance evidence packages for type certification milestones
Collaborate with design engineers to resolve in-flight anomalies by analyzing flight data, identifying root causes, and recommending corrective actions for aircraft systems
Lead pre-flight mission briefings and post-flight debriefs with test pilots, flight crews, and engineering teams to review objectives, results, and lessons learned
